channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Tsitsipas claims first ATP title in 16 months
item.description: Stefanos Tsitsipas wins his first ATP title in 16 months after defeating Raphael Collignon in the Swiss Open final.

item.title: Players will need sex test to play on WTA Tour
item.description: The Women's Tennis Association is to ask all players to undergo genetic sex testing in order to compete on the WTA Tour.

item.title: Muchova to miss Canadian Open after 'small surgery'
item.description: Wimbledon finalist Karolina Muchova says she is set to be sidelined for a "few weeks" after having a "small surgery".

item.title: Female tennis players faced 12,000 abusive posts in 2025
item.description: Female tennis players received 12,000 abusive posts in 2025, according to a new report.

item.title: Alcaraz eyes return from injury at Cincinnati Open
item.description: World number three Carlos Alcaraz enters next month's Cincinnati Open as he plans his return from a wrist injury.

item.title: 'Out of this world!' - Best shots of Wimbledon 2026
item.description: Enjoy the best shots of Wimbledon 2026, featuring Great Britain's Arthur Fery, Novak Djokovic, Coco Gauff and more.

item.title: Sinner beats Zverev in thriller to retain Wimbledon title
item.description: Jannik Sinner comes from a set down to defeat Alexander Zverev 6-7 (7-9) 7-6 (7-2) 6-3 6-4 to retain his Wimbledon title.

item.title: Drama, celebrations and outfits - our end-of-Wimbledon awards
item.description: This year's Wimbledon has been full of excitement, interesting stories and great British success - Naomi Broady runs through her favourite moments.

item.title: The people and places that shaped a two-time Wimbledon champion
item.description: Jannik Sinner is the world's best tennis player. This is the journey that forged a champion.

item.title: From shock loss to Wimbledon title - resilient Sinner rises back to top
item.description: For a brief moment after his shock French Open loss, Jannik Sinner appeared vulnerable. He answered the critics by storming to a second Wimbledon title.

item.title: French player's match-fixing ban increased to three years
item.description: French tennis player Samuel Bensoussan's ban for match fixing has been extended to three years following an unsuccessful appeal, the International Tennis Integrity Agency (ITIA) has confirmed.

item.title: Noskova, an astonishing Wimbledon fightback - and a kiss to the sky
item.description: Linda Noskova produces "one of the all-time greatest efforts on Centre Court" to win Wimbledon - and thanks her late mother, Ivana, for inspiring her.

item.title: How Sinner slid his way to Wimbledon glory
item.description: BBC Sport's Jamie Murray, Tim Henman and Todd Woodbridge analyse Jannik Sinner's Wimbledon final victory against Alexander Zverev.

item.title: Sinner's best shots as world number one retains Wimbledon title
item.description: Enjoy the best of Jannik Sinner's title-retaining performance, as the world number one defeats Alexander Zverev in four sets in the men's singles final at Wimbledon 2026.

item.title: 'It takes two' - Sinner praises Zverev for pushing him in 'amazing' final
item.description: Jannik Sinner says Alexander Zverev can be a Wimbledon champion soon and promises to "be very, very careful" as the German challenges his world number one status, following a four-set win for Sinner in the final of the grass-court Grand Slam.

item.title: Wimbledon crowd 'played big part' in me reaching final - Zverev
item.description: After his defeat to Jannik Sinner, Alexander Zverev reflected on how the Wimbledon crowd "played a big part" in his journey to the final.

item.title: 'Sweet Sinner' wraps up 'compelling' final to retain title
item.description: Jannik Sinner brings Centre Court to its feet, winning a brilliant rally before wrapping up a "compelling" Wimbledon final against Alexander Zverev to retain the men's singles title.

item.title: Sinner holds off Zverev to defend Wimbledon title
item.description: Jannik Sinner underlines why he is the world's best player by holding off Alexander Zverev to win back-to-back Wimbledon men's singles titles.

item.title: Oda beats GB's Hewett in Wimbledon wheelchair singles final
item.description: Great Britain's Alfie Hewett is beaten 6-1 6-1 by top seed Tokito Oda in the final of the men's wheelchair singles at Wimbledon.

item.title: 'Best partner in the world' - GB's Patten and Heliovaara regain Wimbledon title
item.description: Great Britain's Henry Patten and Finnish team-mate Harri Heliovaara claim their second Wimbledon men's doubles title together.

item.title: Noskova holds off extraordinary Muchova fightback to win Wimbledon
item.description: Czech youngster Linda Noskova recovers from missing five championship points to beat Karolina Muchova in an extraordinary Wimbledon final.

item.title: Hewett & Reid win seventh Wimbledon wheelchair doubles title
item.description: British pair Alfie Hewett and Gordon Reid win their seventh Wimbledon men's wheelchair doubles title as a partnership, beating Gustavo Fernandez and Tokito Oda.

item.title: Wimbledon quiz: Name every women's singles champion in the Open era
item.description: It's 58 years since the Open era began in tennis - but can you name every Wimbledon women's singles champion in that time?

item.title: Why Wimbledon is more special to Sinner than other majors
item.description: Jannik Sinner says tennis was only his third sport growing up, behind skiing and football, except during Wimbledon, the one major he watched and the tournament that sparked his love of grass.

item.title: Oda beats GB's Hewett to retain Wimbledon wheelchair title
item.description: Japan's Tokito Oda defeats Great Britain's Alfie Hewett in straight sets to retain his Wimbledon wheelchair men's singles title.

item.title: Why is Zverev allowed to use a phone during his matches?
item.description: Alexander Zverev explains how he uses a mobile phone to monitor his blood glucose levels when he is playing, as the Wimbledon finalist suffers from Type 1 Diabetes.

item.title: Noskova holds her nerve to beat Muchova for first Wimbledon title
item.description: Linda Noskova overcomes missing out on five Championship points in the second set, to defeat Karolina Muchova 6-2 5-7 6-3 to win her first Wimbledon title at 21 years old.

item.title: Tearful Noskova dedicates Wimbledon title to late mother
item.description: Linda Noskova says "all the blood, sweat and tears was worth it" after the 21-year old defeated her Czech compatriot Karolina Muchova in the 2026 women's singles final at Wimbledon.

item.title: 'It's a beauty!' - best shots from day 13 at Wimbledon
item.description: Enjoy the best shots from day 13 at Wimbledon featuring Great Britain's wheelchair doubles champions Gordon Reid and Alfie Hewett, as well as the new women's singles champion Linda Noskova.

item.title: 'Disappointed' Muchova will 'fight' to be back after Wimbledon loss
item.description: Karolina Muchova expresses her appreciation to her friends and family and the Centre Court crowd for their support, following her defeat to Linda Noskova in the 2026 Wimbledon women's singles final.

item.title: Noskova recovers from five missed championship points to beat Muchova
item.description: Linda Noskova recovers from missing five championship points in the second set to take the deciding set 6-3 to win her first Wimbledon title.

item.title: GB's Patten wins second Wimbledon doubles title with Heliovaara
item.description: Britain's Henry Patten wins his second Wimbledon men's doubles title alongside Harri Heliovaara of Finland, defeating Marcelo Arevalo and Mate Pavic 7-6 (7-4) 7-6 (7-3).

item.title: GB's Hewett & Reid roar back to win seventh doubles title
item.description: Great Britain's Gordon Reid and Alfie Hewett come from a set down against Gustavo Fernandez and Tokito Oda to win a seventh Wimbledon men's wheelchair doubles title.

item.title: Superb Sinner ends Djokovic's latest bid for history
item.description: Jannik Sinner produces a devastating display to end Novak Djokovic's latest bid for history and set up a Wimbledon final against Alexander Zverev.

item.title: Fery's Wimbledon run ended by Zverev in semi-finals
item.description: Arthur Fery's unforgettable Wimbledon run comes to an end as Alexander Zverev beats the British wildcard to secure a place in the final.

item.title: What is the difference between quad and wheelchair tennis?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team answer your questions on wheelchair tennis at Wimbledon.

item.title: 'Good but not good enough' for 'blessed and cursed' Djokovic
item.description: Most players would be satisfied with reaching a Wimbledon semi-final and the Australian Open final in the space of six months. Not Novak Djokovic.

item.title: Why are Wimbledon's colours purple and green?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the history of Wimbledon's colour scheme.

item.title: To Wimbledon and back - Fery's voyage to Centre Court spotlight
item.description: Arthur Fery learned how to play tennis about a mile away from Wimbledon's Centre Court - now he's one win from the SW19 final.

item.title: 'The whole match was the tie-break' - how rollercoaster semi-final unfolded
item.description: Twenty-two points, 18 minutes, one winner - how Karolina Muchova and Coco Gauff's "rollercoaster" semi-final was decided by an astonishing tie-break.

item.title: The man who invented a sport and played tennis with Willy Wonka
item.description: Brad Parks came up with the idea for a new sport while on a family picnic 50 years ago.

item.title: When five Wimbledon titles in one weekend changed British tennis
item.description: A decade after Andy Murray's 2016 men's singles title, we look at the legacy of the golden weekend where five Wimbledon trophies were won by British players.

item.title: Battling egos and stereotypes - the rise of female tennis coaches
item.description: In a sport like tennis, which prides itself on gender equality, why are there still so few female coaches and what is being done to address the issue?

item.title: Anisimova plots Wimbledon sequel with happier ending
item.description: Many wondered how long it would take Amanda Anisimova to recover from last year's Wimbledon final. About six weeks was the answer.

item.title: Williams is the greatest - but will she produce another great fight?
item.description: Serena Williams will return to the singles court at Wimbledon aged 44 - BBC Sport analyses if she is ready and the challenges she will face.

item.title: How Murray was tempted back to tennis - and would he ever do a Serena?
item.description: British tennis icon Andy Murray discusses returning to coaching with Jack Draper, Serena Williams' comeback and filling the void of not playing.

item.title: 'I never imagined something like this' - Nadal reflects on 2008 Wimbledon win
item.description: Rafael Nadal recalls his seismic five-set victory over Roger Federer in the 2008 Wimbledon final, arguably the greatest men's match ever played.

item.title: What are the heat rules at Wimbledon?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the heat rules at Wimbledon and when it is and is not safe to play in the heat

item.title: Who are the Wimbledon record-holders?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team looks at who has won the most Wimbledon singles and doubles titles.

item.title: What do hand signals mean in Wimbledon doubles?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team explains what the player hand signals mean in doubles matches.

item.title: What is Wimbledon's Last 8 Club?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at what Wimbledon's Last 8 Club is and how players qualify for it.

item.title: How is the Wimbledon schedule decided? Your questions answered
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team answer your questions about the Wimbledon schedule.
